title: Tenant Groups Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the tenant groups capability, derived from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage and not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facets cite verified manifest source rows as lineage; proof is hermetic/stale and not current readiness. Do not treat as implementation authority or fresh proof; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Group lifecycle is list existing groups, create after name validation and a duplicate check, update after existence and conflict checks, and delete after a protected-name guard. Each mutation runs its precondition checks before touching the provider.

stateModel.description: State is a tenant-realm group identified by id, name, and path, with reserved names protected; groups live in the identity provider's realm and are not persisted in the relational store. The group set is scoped to the tenant realm.

permissions.description: Routes require the specific group read, create, update, or delete permission, are tenant-scoped, and declare a group resource and matching scopes, resolving the tenant realm credential from context. One tenant cannot manage another tenant's groups.

contracts.description: The operation surface lists and creates groups and updates or deletes a group by id, backed by the tenant-credential realm admin adapter. Mutations return typed results mapped to standard responses.

validation.description: Group names must be non-empty strings of at most 64 characters with no path separators or control characters, must not be reserved, and must not duplicate an existing name case-insensitively. Invalid names are rejected before any provider write.

errorModel.description: Typed outcomes include invalid-name, conflict, not-found, protected, and ok, mapped fail-closed to 400/409/404/422/201/204, and a missing tenant credential returns a no-credential/503 result; provider failure keeps mutation boundaries closed. No partial group state is left behind.

auditModel.description: Create, update, and delete emit group-created, group-updated, and group-deleted audit-before-change events after precondition checks, and audit failure aborts the provider mutation. Read-only listing is not audited.

readinessModel.description: Semantic readiness is invariant-ready, while route execution additionally requires tenant context and the tenant-realm auth-settings credential, and degraded mode disables provider-dependent mutation while keeping read-only readiness visible. A UI semantic harness proves the generation contract without shipping a legacy page.

proof.description: A unit proof covers group lifecycle and validation and a UI semantic harness proves the headless journey, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic behaviour/state validation plus a semantic harness for future UI generation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; the source-lineage headless group journey is lineage only. Author the UI semantic model before generation.
